data.headline: Kitchen Remodeling in Orange, CT
data.subheadline: Licensed Connecticut kitchen remodeling contractor serving Orange and New Haven County , full-service renovations from $30,000 to $150,000+.

data.title: Full-Service Kitchen Remodeling in Orange, CT
data.content:
Kitchen remodeling in Orange typically ranges from $30,000 for a basic cabinet and countertop update to $150,000 or more for a full custom renovation with structural changes. Most projects take six to twelve weeks of active construction, plus cabinet lead time. We hold CT HIC License #0668405 and serve Orange through our New Haven County Service Area Team.

Orange is our home base , we operate from 206A Boston Post Road. The housing stock is primarily split-levels, colonials, capes, and ranch homes built between the 1950s and 1980s. Many of these kitchens are original to the home or have had only cosmetic updates. Undersized electrical panels, galvanized plumbing, and compartmentalized layouts are the norm.

Orange homeowners expect clear proposals, daily updates during construction, and a project that finishes on the schedule we quoted. That's exactly how we operate.

data.title: What Is Included in a Kitchen Remodel in Orange
data.content:
A full kitchen remodel in Orange covers every trade from demolition through final paint: cabinetry, countertops, tile, flooring, electrical, plumbing, appliance installation, and all required Orange Building Department permits and inspections. Orange is primarily a 1950s through 1970s ranch town, and the compact rectangular kitchens in those homes are among the most common starting points we work from, often with homeowners wanting to remove the wall separating the kitchen from the dining room to create an open layout.

We start with demolition and removal of existing cabinets, countertops, and flooring. Before any new material goes in, we assess what is behind the walls. Orange ranches from the 1950s and 1960s were built on a consistent template, and that template regularly includes single-wall kitchen electrical runs that cannot support today's appliance load, original copper supply lines that are in good condition, and load-bearing walls between the kitchen and dining area that require a beam and post when removed. We confirm the structural scope before any framing comes down.

New cabinetry installation includes soft-close hardware and adjustable shelving as standard. In Orange open-concept remodels where the kitchen expands into the former dining room, we size the new cabinet run and island to fit the combined space rather than simply extending the old layout. Countertop fabrication and installation, backsplash tile, and underlayment preparation are all part of the project. Electrical scope covers new circuits for appliances, under-cabinet lighting, and updated outlet and switch locations.

Plumbing includes sink and dishwasher connections, garbage disposal installation, and any relocated water lines. When a sink moves to a new island position in an Orange ranch, we run the drain and supply through the crawl space, which is accessible in most of these homes. We coordinate appliance delivery and installation so you are not managing that separately.

Interior painting, drywall patching, and daily cleanup are included throughout. We install dust barriers to protect the rest of your home during construction. The project closes with a final walkthrough where we go through every detail together.

data.title: What Makes Kitchen Remodeling in Orange Different
data.content: As Orange's local remodeling contractor, we know this housing stock better than anyone. We've completed numerous kitchen renovations within walking distance of our office. Orange homeowners tend to be practical, value-oriented, and want honest communication about what their project will cost and how long it will take. That's exactly how we operate.

data.title: Orange Building Department and Permits
data.content: The Orange Building Department is located at Town Hall on Orange Center Road. Kitchen renovations involving structural, electrical, or plumbing work require permits. As a local Orange business, we have a strong working relationship with the building department. Permit review times are typically 1-2 weeks , faster than most surrounding towns.

data.title: Kitchen Remodeling Cost in Orange, CT
data.subtitle: Kitchen remodeling in Orange ranges from $30,000 for a basic cabinet and countertop refresh to $150,000 or more for a full custom renovation with structural changes. Ranges are illustrative for typical Connecticut projects. Final pricing depends on scope, materials, site conditions, and permit fees. Detailed written estimate provided before any work begins.

data.title: Why Orange Kitchen Remodeling Requires Local Expertise
data.content:
Orange homes present construction conditions that contractors without local experience regularly miss.

Orange kitchens present familiar conditions: 100-amp electrical panels that can't support modern appliance loads, galvanized plumbing ready for replacement, and layouts designed when kitchens were utilitarian spaces rather than the center of the home.

Split-level homes , extremely common in Orange , have unique kitchen challenges with level changes and structural considerations. We've renovated dozens of Orange split-level kitchens and know exactly what to expect.

data.title: Our Kitchen Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: Every Orange kitchen remodel follows the same five-step process. This structure keeps projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.short: Visit your Orange home or connect by video
steps.description: We visit your Orange home or connect via Google Meet or Zoom to discuss your goals, assess the space, and answer your questions. No charge. No obligation. We note the existing electrical and plumbing conditions upfront.

steps.title: Planning
steps.short: Clear written proposal and line items
steps.description: You receive a clear written proposal covering exactly what's included, how long it will take, and what it costs. We break out cabinetry, countertops, tile, electrical, plumbing, flooring, and Orange Building Department permits separately so you understand exactly where the budget is going.

steps.title: Selections
steps.short: Materials and lead times locked in early
steps.description: We guide you through material choices with options at different price points. We communicate lead times clearly so selections are made on schedule before construction begins. If a material has a long lead time, we flag that immediately.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed schedule. You receive daily updates on progress, a clean job site at the end of every workday, and crews who arrive when scheduled. If something unexpected comes up behind a wall, we contact you that day and present your options.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished project together. We check every cabinet, every drawer, every tile, every light fixture. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete. Your written acceptance at the final walkthrough is the last step.

data.title: Project Timeline
data.subtitle: Most kitchen remodels take ten to twenty-two weeks from signed proposal to final walkthrough.

items.title: Planning & Design
items.description: 1-2 Weeks. Consultation, measurements, material selections, and detailed proposal.

items.title: Cabinet Lead Time
items.description: 4-12 Weeks. Custom orders require the longest lead time. Semi-custom options arrive in four to six weeks.

items.title: Construction
items.description: 6-12 Weeks. Demo, rough-ins, cabinetry, countertops, tile, flooring, and fixtures.

items.title: Final Touches
items.description: 1 Week. Hardware, final connections, punch list, and walkthrough.

items.question: Do I need a permit for kitchen remodeling in Orange?
items.answer: Yes. Most kitchen remodeling projects in Orange require permits from the Orange Building Department, particularly when the work involves electrical, plumbing, or structural modifications. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project; you do not need to manage that process yourself.

items.question: How much does kitchen remodeling cost in Orange?
items.answer: Kitchen remodeling in Orange typically ranges from $30,000 for a basic cabinet and countertop refresh to $150,000 or more for a full custom renovation with structural changes. A mid-range project with new cabinets, stone countertops, new flooring, and updated lighting generally falls between $60,000 and $100,000. Your written proposal includes a detailed cost breakdown by trade so you know exactly where the budget is going.

items.question: How long does a kitchen remodel take in Orange?
items.answer: Active construction on a Orange kitchen remodel takes six to twelve weeks depending on the scope of work. Cabinet lead time needs to be added to that figure: custom cabinets require eight to twelve weeks, semi-custom four to six weeks, and stock cabinets are available within one to two weeks. Your written proposal includes a complete project schedule that accounts for all lead times.

items.question: What types of homes do you remodel kitchens in for Orange?
items.answer: Orange's housing stock is primarily split-levels, colonials, capes, and ranch homes built between the 1950s through 1980s. We're familiar with the construction conditions common in these homes and account for them in every proposal and project scope.

items.question: What is included in a kitchen remodel in Orange?
items.answer: A full-scope kitchen remodel in Orange includes demolition and removal of all existing cabinetry, countertops, and flooring; structural assessment; new cabinetry installation with hardware; countertop fabrication and installation; backsplash tile work; new flooring; all electrical work including new circuits and lighting; all plumbing including sink, dishwasher, and disposal connections; appliance coordination and installation; interior painting; all drywall patching and finish work; Orange Building Department permit applications and inspection coordination; daily cleanup; and a final walkthrough.
